author: Corpus Analyst Rowan-C

dimensionSummaries.dimension.player-fantasy: The player is an improvising explorer-engineer who rebuilds capability from found matter and links sky, surface, caves, and depths into self-made routes.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.core-loop: The cadence is to identify a destination or need, gather nearby materials and devices, construct or fuse a solution, traverse one of the world's layers, and convert discovery into stronger future improvisation.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.verbs: Climbing, gliding, fighting, cooking, and gathering return alongside Ultrahand assembly, Fuse, Recall, Ascend, device activation, blueprint recreation, throwing, and material-enhanced archery.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.world-state: Built objects, moved geometry, lit roots, completed shrines and quests, cleared phenomena, map discovery, weapon fusions, environmental time reversal, and temporary devices operate at several persistence scales.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.topology: A vertically layered Hyrule connects sky islands, surface terrain, caves, wells, chasms, and the depths through falls, launches, Ascend surfaces, holes, towers, and player-built vehicles.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.gating-revisit: An opening sky island teaches core powers, while later access is often governed by knowledge, preparation, vertical entry, environmental resistance, devices, regional story state, or newly gained support.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.puzzle-information: Loose components, attachment points, device affordances, motion histories, overhead surfaces, material effects, and example constructions expose a combinatorial rule grammar.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.dungeon-structure: Shrines isolate power experiments, regional temples embed large traversal devices in their surrounding quests, caves create compact discoveries, and the depths provide an extended environmental challenge layer.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.exploration-reward: Exploration yields shrines, roots, caves, armor, blueprints, devices, energy resources, weapons, schema stones, quests, maps, materials, and vantage points across mutually informative layers.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.growth-economy: Hearts, stamina, battery capacity, armor, inventory slots, Zonai devices, zonaite, charges, rupees, meals, materials, and fused weapon durability support multiple build and survival economies.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.combat-boss: Combat combines direct weapon timing with fused properties, arrow materials, deployable devices, constructed weapons, stealth, ally support, environmental manipulation, and retreat.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.npc-quest: Regional partners, settlement residents, researchers, travelers, builders, newspaper investigations, and stable networks turn the Upheaval into many local problems and unlock services or abilities.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.narrative-performance: The missing-Zelda mystery unfolds through regional action, discovered memories, present-day investigations, and companion vows, alternating broad agency with compact performed revelations.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.camera-blocking: A third-person camera supports horizon and vertical-layer scouting, close assembly inspection, attachment rotation, object highlighting, aiming, companion readability, and cinematic reveals.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.teaching-feedback: The opening island stages powers in controlled shrines, while ghosted attachments, glue visuals, motion trails, valid Ascend surfaces, material previews, battery meters, and device behavior explain experiments.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.ui-input: Radial power choice, inventory sorting, quick material attachment, map layers, quest records, battery and stamina meters, device capsules, blueprints, and pins expose a large combinatorial vocabulary.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.recovery-accessibility: Frequent saves, fast travel, retreat, alternate construction, detachable or replaceable materials, cooking, roots that heal gloom damage, and optional hints support recovery from failed routes and builds.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.perspective-implementation: The 3D presentation makes volume, orientation, altitude, overhangs, falling, construction, and cross-layer sightlines central; reliable camera and physics feedback are therefore prerequisites.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.hardware-multiplayer: The original is a single-player Switch game with controller and optional motion aiming; creation is locally simulated rather than synchronized, though later editions can share blueprint data externally.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.production-runtime: Its principles demand robust object attachment, rigid-body simulation, device energy, reversible motion histories, vertical streaming, construction persistence rules, AI interaction, save safety, and combinatorial testing.

observationThemes.context: A gap, transport problem, hostile space, or shrine presents loose components and devices rather than one completed mechanism.
preconditions: At least two movable or attachable components are present
The obstacle permits a physical construction response
playerInput: Activate, steer, or reposition the result
before: No usable bridge, vehicle, or mechanism spans the obstacle
action: Assemble a purpose-built object and test it in the local physics context
after: The construction functions, fails visibly, or can be revised
The route or encounter gains a new option
feedback: Highlighting, orientation controls, attachment points, adhesive visuals, and motion show assembly state
Physical behavior reveals balance, thrust, weight, and contact consequences
consequences: The player can iterate instead of searching only for a prescribed key
The runtime must remain stable across many unanticipated component arrangements
observationThemes.factualDescription: Ultrahand lets the player move, rotate, and attach world objects and Zonai devices, then activate the resulting construction to address traversal, puzzle, or combat needs.
observationThemes.interpretation: Providing parts plus legible physical constraints turns authored obstacles into local design problems, with failure serving as information for the next revision.

observationThemes.context: A destination on the surface, sky, cave system, or depths can be approached through a launch, fall, chasm, overhang, Ascend surface, or player-built vehicle.
preconditions: The destination or useful vertical relationship has been identified
A viable entry method and sufficient preparation are available
playerInput: Use Ascend, glide, fall, launch, climb, or construct a vehicle
before: The target is separated by altitude or an opaque boundary
The current layer's ordinary path is blocked or inefficient
action: Traverse the vertical edge between world layers
after: The player occupies a new layer or side of the boundary
New landmarks, resources, or return routes become available
feedback: Altitude, camera framing, map layers, falling distance, ceilings, and transition effects make the move legible
The destination's geometry aligns with the chosen entry
consequences: Old locations gain new approaches when a higher or lower layer is understood
Exploration cycles between layers rather than treating each as an isolated map
observationThemes.factualDescription: Sky islands, surface terrain, caves, chasms, and the depths are connected by vertical travel methods including gliding, falling, Ascend, towers, and constructed devices.
observationThemes.interpretation: A layered world becomes one navigational system when vertical edges are numerous, readable, and useful for revisiting known coordinates from a different altitude.

observationThemes.context: The player has a base weapon, shield, or arrow and a material whose properties could answer an enemy, terrain, durability, or range problem.
preconditions: Fuse or arrow attachment is available
Compatible equipment and a material are in reach or inventory
before: The base equipment has its ordinary power and durability
The material is only loose loot or inventory
action: Bind the material property to the equipment slot
after: The equipment gains altered power, durability, geometry, element, or utility
The material is consumed into the fused state
feedback: The combined object is visibly represented
Inventory values, previews, hit effects, and enemy or terrain reactions expose the new function
consequences: Enemy drops become inputs for the next encounter
Players can recover weak equipment by making context-specific tools instead of waiting for fixed upgrades
observationThemes.factualDescription: Fuse combines one material or object with a weapon or shield, and materials can be attached to arrows, producing visible equipment with changed combat or utility properties.
observationThemes.interpretation: A reversible-looking but resource-consuming combination rule can turn ordinary loot into situational verbs and connect combat rewards directly to future experimentation.

observationThemes.context: In the depths, darkness and gloom obscure terrain while a distant root offers light, map revelation, and recovery.
preconditions: The player has entered an unlit region of the depths
A light source, visible root, or navigational hypothesis is available
playerInput: Deploy brightbloom light or travel toward the root
before: Terrain and threats are difficult to read
Gloom damage has limited ordinary heart recovery
action: Create temporary light and reach the persistent illumination node
after: The area around the root is lit and mapped
Gloom-damaged hearts can be restored at the root
feedback: Darkness recedes, map data appears, and health state changes
The root becomes a stable landmark in the extended challenge layer
consequences: Exploration becomes safer and future routes more legible
A risky expedition yields both knowledge and a recovery anchor
observationThemes.factualDescription: The depths begin in darkness; player-created light supports movement, while activating a Lightroot illuminates and maps an area and restores hearts affected by gloom.
observationThemes.interpretation: A hostile exploration layer can reward progress with a node that simultaneously improves perception, topology, and recovery, making danger reduction itself a discovery reward.

observationThemes.context: A regional crisis is investigated and resolved alongside a local partner who acts during traversal, scenes, and the area's temple climax.
preconditions: The partner and their contextual ability are available
playerInput: Travel and solve objectives with the partner
Invoke the partner ability at relevant obstacles and combat states
before: The partner's support is local and story-bound
action: Coordinate with the companion through the quest and boss sequence
after: A persistent vow enables a related support ability beyond the region
feedback: Companion placement, prompts, voice, action effects, scenes, and environmental change show the partnership's effect
The camera reframes major joint actions and resolution beats
consequences: Narrative relationship becomes ongoing mechanical support
The original remains single-player, so companion behavior is AI-controlled rather than synchronized with another human
observationThemes.factualDescription: Regional quests pair Link with local companions whose abilities support traversal and combat; after the regional climax, a vow provides a persistent related ability.
observationThemes.interpretation: A companion arc gains continuity when its climactic relationship reward remains callable in later play, turning performed cooperation into durable agency.

contributionHypotheses: Construction systems can turn obstacles into iterative design problems when parts, constraints, and failure feedback remain locally legible.
Several world layers feel integrated when their vertical edges create useful approaches to the same horizontal coordinates.
Loot gains systemic meaning when materials can be converted into visible properties on ordinary equipment.
A dangerous exploration region can reward advancement with nodes that combine illumination, mapping, and recovery.
uniqueOrNonportable: Hyrule's layered map, Zonai culture and devices, named arm powers, exact constructions, characters, temples, creatures, story scenes, iconography, music, and dialogue are nonportable.
Specific shrine and temple solutions, device recipes, blueprint shapes, boss weaknesses, layer correspondence, and resource tables remain evidence only.
Portable construction principles must be re-expressed with an original part vocabulary, world fiction, silhouettes, constraints, and audiovisual feedback.
openQuestions: A later version delta should isolate Nintendo Switch 2 Edition performance and Zelda Notes blueprint-sharing effects from original single-player design evidence.
